title: sorbus domestica var. lanuginosa
intro: Sorbus domestica var. lanuginosa, also known as the downy whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is native to parts of Europe and Asia, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's bark, leaves, and berries contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that are known to have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Sorbus domestica var. lanuginosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
